Tests the behavior of zero character handling in JavaScript string operations.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that evaluates JavaScript expressions to verify specific behaviors related to string handling and the `eval` function. It attempts to execute a string containing a null character using `eval`, which should not throw an error. If an error occurs, the `catch` block will trigger an assertion failure. Additionally, the script contains an assertion to confirm that two strings with different characters after a null character are not equal. This script provides narrow functionality focused on testing string comparison and `eval` behavior with null characters.
